steps.title: Rewrite with common denominator
steps.content:
To combine the fractions, rewrite each with the common denominator $(4x-5)(x+1)$:

$$\frac{4}{4x-5} - \frac{1}{x+1} = \frac{4(x+1)}{(4x-5)(x+1)} - \frac{4x-5}{(x+1)(4x-5)}$$

steps.title: Combine numerators and simplify
steps.content:
Subtract the numerators over the common denominator:

$$\frac{4(x+1) - (4x-5)}{(4x-5)(x+1)}$$

Expand and simplify:

$$\frac{4x + 4 - 4x + 5}{(4x-5)(x+1)} = \frac{9}{(x+1)(4x-5)}$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
